sanskrit: स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः । भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥
hindiTranslation: तू मेरा भक्त और प्रिय सखा है, इसलिये वही यह पुरातन योग आज मैंने तुझसे कहा है क्योंकि यह बड़ा उत्तम रहस्य है ॥
englishTranslation: Because you are my devotee and my friend, I have taught you today this ancient yoga. It is a supreme secret.
essence: The deepest teaching opens only to devotion and trust.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he is teaching Arjuna an ancient teaching because Arjuna is both devoted and close to him. This teaching is a rare and highest secret.
simpleInsight: Closeness opens what argument cannot. Trust makes room for the deepest teaching.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still stunned by Krishna's earlier claim that this teaching began long ago. Krishna now explains why it is being given here: not to everyone, but to Arjuna, who is both devoted and a trusted friend. That relationship makes the hidden teaching possible.
modernRelevance: A mentor finally tells you the hard truth after years of trust, not because you asked perfectly, but because you are ready to hear it.
translationLiteral: सः (that) / एव (indeed) / अयम् (this) / मया (by me) / ते (to you) / अद्य (today) / योगः (yoga) / प्रोक्तः (spoken) / पुरातनः (ancient) / भक्तः (devotee) / असि (you are) / मे (my) / सखा (friend) / च (and) / इति (thus) / रहस्यम् (secret) / हि (indeed) / एतत् (this) / उत्तमम् (supreme)
insight: It shifts the mind from demanding proof to recognising that deep understanding ripens through trust and relationship.
